title: The 11 Best Marketing Automation Software (2026)
subtitle: A comprehensive ranking of the top platforms for automating marketing workflows, lead nurturing, and customer engagement across B2B, B2C, and e-commerce.
audience: Marketing managers choosing marketing automation platforms (HubSpot vs Marketo vs ActiveCampaign vs Klaviyo)

editor.name: Top 11 Editorial
editor.credential: Autonomous AI ranking engine — methodology v1.0 weights public
editor.conflict_disclosure: None. The editor of Top 11 is not a candidate on this list.

independence.statement: Top 11 takes no payment from any provider on this list. Scores are computed from a public weighted rubric; methodology weights were locked before entry research began.

changelog.text: Initial publication. Methodology v1.0 weights Automation & Workflow Builder (30%), CRM & Lead Management (25%), Integrations & Ecosystem (20%), Analytics & Reporting (15%), and Usability & Support (10%).

answer_capsule: The best marketing automation software is HubSpot for its all-in-one CRM platform, followed by ActiveCampaign for its powerful automation and segmentation, and Klaviyo for e-commerce.

criteria.name: Automation & Workflow Builder
criteria.description: Power, flexibility, and intuitiveness of the automation rules, visual builders, and logic (e.g., branching, A/B testing, triggers).

criteria.name: CRM & Lead Management
criteria.description: Effectiveness of contact management, lead scoring, segmentation, list management, and native CRM capabilities or deep integration.

criteria.name: Integrations & Ecosystem
criteria.description: Breadth and depth of native integrations with other marketing tools (CMS, e-commerce, social, ads) and API accessibility.

criteria.name: Analytics & Reporting
criteria.description: Quality of dashboards, campaign performance tracking, revenue attribution, and custom reporting capabilities.

criteria.name: Usability & Support
criteria.description: Ease of use for non-technical marketers, quality of the user interface, and responsiveness/availability of customer support.

solves: scaling marketing efforts without adding headcount
aligning marketing and sales in large teams

guide.a: Marketing automation is the use of software to automate repetitive marketing tasks. This includes sending emails, nurturing leads, posting to social media, and tracking campaign performance. The goal is to make marketing efforts more efficient, personalized, and effective at scale.

guide.q: How does it differ from a CRM?
guide.a: A CRM (Customer Relationship Management) system is primarily a database for managing customer and prospect information and interactions. Marketing automation software focuses on executing and automating marketing campaigns. While many platforms (like HubSpot) combine both, a pure marketing automation tool uses CRM data to trigger automated marketing actions.

guide.q: What are the key features to look for?
guide.a: Look for a powerful visual workflow builder, robust email marketing capabilities, lead scoring and grading, dynamic segmentation, A/B testing, native integrations with your existing tools (especially your CRM and e-commerce platform), and detailed analytics with revenue attribution.

how_to_choose: Assess your primary use case: Are you B2B (long sales cycles), e-commerce (transaction-focused), or a content publisher? Your business model dictates the features you'll need most.
Evaluate your existing tech stack: Choose a platform that integrates seamlessly with your CRM, CMS, and other critical software to avoid data silos.

Consider your team's technical skill: Enterprise platforms like Marketo offer immense power but have a steep learning curve. Platforms like HubSpot or Mailchimp are more user-friendly for teams without dedicated marketing ops specialists.

Start with your contact list size and budget: Pricing is almost always tied to the number of contacts in your database. Calculate your total cost of ownership, including any setup fees and potential overages.

faqs.q: What is the best marketing automation software for small businesses?
faqs.a: For small businesses, ActiveCampaign often hits the sweet spot of power and affordability. HubSpot's free and starter tiers are also excellent entry points, and Mailchimp is a very user-friendly option for those primarily focused on email.

faqs.q: Which is better: HubSpot or Marketo?
faqs.a: HubSpot is generally better for companies seeking an all-in-one, user-friendly platform that combines marketing, sales, and service tools. Marketo is a more powerful, specialized B2B marketing automation engine ideal for enterprise companies with complex needs and the resources to manage it.

faqs.q: How much does marketing automation software cost?
faqs.a: Costs vary dramatically. Expect to pay anywhere from $50/month for a basic plan with a small contact list to over $5,000/month for enterprise-level platforms. Most providers use a tiered pricing model based on the number of contacts and feature set.

faqs.q: Can marketing automation replace my email marketing tool?
faqs.a: Yes, in almost all cases. Marketing automation platforms have email marketing as a core component but add sophisticated workflows, segmentation, and multi-channel capabilities that go far beyond what a standard email marketing tool can do.

honest_disclosures: Pricing for enterprise-level tools (e.g., Marketo, Pardot) is often not public and requires a custom quote, making direct cost comparison difficult.
This list has a strong representation of US-based companies, which may affect the depth of support and localization for non-US markets.
The term 'all-in-one' can be misleading. While some platforms offer broad functionality, you will likely still need specialized tools for things like SEO or advanced social media management.

glossary.definition: The process of developing and reinforcing relationships with buyers at every stage of the sales funnel. It involves using marketing automation to send relevant, personalized content and communications to prospects over time, guiding them towards a purchase.

entries.name: HubSpot
entries.best_for: Businesses of all sizes seeking a powerful, user-friendly, all-in-one platform that tightly integrates marketing, sales, and service.
entries.verdict: HubSpot ranks #1 because its tightly integrated CRM and user-friendly interface make it the most complete and accessible marketing automation platform on the market.
entries.verdict_short: The best all-in-one platform with an exceptional CRM and user experience, ideal for scaling businesses.
entries.praise: The free CRM is best-in-class, and the platform's educational resources (HubSpot Academy) are unparalleled for training new team members.
entries.praise_short: Unbeatable free CRM and educational resources.
entries.criticism: Costs can escalate quickly as your contact list grows, and advanced features are often locked into expensive bundles.
entries.criticism_short: Pricing scales aggressively with contact list size.
sources_pending: Forrester Wave: B2B Marketing Automation Platforms

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-07.

entries.name: ActiveCampaign
entries.best_for: SMBs and mid-market companies that need exceptionally powerful and flexible automation capabilities without an enterprise price tag.
entries.verdict: ActiveCampaign earns its #2 spot by offering the most powerful and intuitive visual automation builder in its class, making sophisticated customer journeys accessible to non-enterprise users.
entries.verdict_short: Unmatched automation power and flexibility at a competitive price point, perfect for sophisticated SMB marketers.
entries.praise: The 'if/then/else' logic in the workflow builder allows for incredibly granular personalization and segmentation.
entries.praise_short: Extremely granular and flexible workflow builder.
entries.criticism: The built-in CRM is functional but not as robust as dedicated solutions, and reporting could be more advanced.
entries.criticism_short: CRM and reporting are less robust than competitors.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-07.

entries.name: Klaviyo
entries.best_for: E-commerce businesses, especially those on Shopify, that want to leverage deep customer data for highly personalized marketing automation.
entries.verdict: Klaviyo is the #3 choice and the undisputed leader for e-commerce marketing automation due to its incredibly deep, native integrations with platforms like Shopify and its focus on revenue-driving analytics.
entries.verdict_short: The gold standard for e-commerce, with deep data integration and powerful segmentation for online stores.
entries.praise: Its ability to pull in and segment based on granular e-commerce events (e.g., 'viewed product,' 'added to cart') is second to none.
entries.praise_short: Unmatched e-commerce event tracking and segmentation.
entries.criticism: The platform is less suited for B2B companies with long sales cycles, and its pricing can become expensive for stores with large email lists.
entries.criticism_short: Highly specialized for e-commerce; less ideal for B2B.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-07.

entries.name: Adobe Marketo Engage
entries.best_for: Enterprise B2B organizations that require a deeply powerful, customizable, and scalable platform for complex, multi-touch customer journeys.
entries.verdict: Marketo secures the #4 position as the go-to choice for enterprises needing immense power and customizability, offering unparalleled depth in lead management and campaign execution.
entries.verdict_short: An incredibly powerful and customizable enterprise platform, but with a steep learning curve and high price.
entries.praise: Its capabilities in lead scoring, revenue cycle modeling, and sales partnership are robust enough for the most demanding global companies.
entries.praise_short: Unmatched depth for enterprise lead management.
entries.criticism: The platform is notoriously complex, has a dated user interface, and requires significant technical expertise and resources to manage effectively.
entries.criticism_short: Very complex with a steep learning curve.
sources_pending: Gartner Magic Quadrant for B2B Marketing Automation

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-07.

entries.name: Salesforce Marketing Cloud Account Engagement (Pardot)
entries.best_for: B2B companies that are heavily invested in the Salesforce ecosystem and want seamless alignment between their marketing and sales teams.
entries.verdict: Pardot (now Account Engagement) is ranked #5 because its native integration with Salesforce CRM is the tightest in the industry, making it the default choice for B2B marketers aiming for perfect sales and marketing alignment.
entries.verdict_short: The best choice for B2B teams on Salesforce, offering unmatched CRM integration for sales alignment.
entries.praise: The bidirectional sync with Salesforce is flawless, providing sales teams with incredible visibility into marketing engagement.
entries.criticism: The platform can feel clunky and less intuitive than modern competitors, and its value diminishes significantly if you are not using Salesforce CRM.
entries.criticism_short: Less intuitive UI; value is tied to Salesforce CRM.
sources_pending: Forrester Wave: B2B Marketing Automation Platforms

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-07.

entries.name: Customer.io
entries.best_for: Tech, SaaS, and mobile-app companies that need to trigger messages based on real-time user behavior and complex event data.
entries.verdict: Customer.io stands out at #6 for its developer-friendly, API-first approach, allowing product and marketing teams to build highly sophisticated, event-triggered messaging campaigns across email, push, and SMS.
entries.verdict_short: A flexible, API-first platform for tech companies to automate messaging based on user behavior.
entries.praise: Its data-handling flexibility and segmentation based on real-time event data are extremely powerful for product-led growth strategies.
entries.criticism: It's less of an all-in-one solution and requires more technical setup than platforms like HubSpot; not ideal for traditional B2B lead nurturing.
entries.criticism_short: Requires technical setup; not an all-in-one tool.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-07.

entries.name: GetResponse
entries.best_for: Small businesses and entrepreneurs looking for an affordable, all-in-one marketing platform that includes automation, webinars, and website building.
entries.verdict: GetResponse earns the #7 spot by bundling a surprisingly wide range of features—including webinar hosting and landing page builders—into a single, cost-effective package ideal for small businesses.
entries.verdict_short: A budget-friendly, feature-rich platform including unique tools like webinar hosting for small businesses.
entries.praise: The inclusion of a full webinar marketing solution is a unique and high-value feature at its price point.
entries.praise_short: Includes a full webinar marketing solution.
entries.criticism: While it does many things, the depth of its automation and CRM features doesn't match more specialized platforms.
entries.criticism_short: Features lack the depth of specialized competitors.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-07.

entries.name: Mailchimp
entries.best_for: Small businesses and creators graduating from simple email newsletters to basic, user-friendly marketing automation.
entries.verdict: Mailchimp is ranked #8 because it provides the gentlest entry point into marketing automation, leveraging its best-in-class usability and familiar interface for users moving beyond basic email campaigns.
entries.verdict_short: The easiest platform to get started with, perfect for beginners graduating from simple email marketing.
entries.praise: Its user interface and email editor are exceptionally intuitive and well-designed, making it easy to create beautiful campaigns.
entries.praise_short: Exceptionally intuitive user interface and email editor.
entries.criticism: The automation capabilities are limited compared to competitors, and the pricing becomes uncompetitive as your contact list and feature needs grow.
entries.criticism_short: Limited automation and poor value at scale.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-07.

entries.name: Brevo (formerly Sendinblue)
entries.best_for: Budget-conscious small businesses needing a suite of marketing and sales tools, including automation, transactional email, and live chat.
entries.verdict: Brevo's position at #9 is secured by its aggressive, value-focused pricing, offering a broad suite of tools including SMS, chat, and transactional emails that are often expensive add-ons elsewhere.
entries.verdict_short: An excellent value proposition, bundling automation with chat, SMS, and a generous free tier.
entries.praise: The free tier is one of the most generous on the market, and its pricing model is based on email volume rather than contacts, which can be very cost-effective.
entries.praise_short: Generous free tier and volume-based pricing.
entries.criticism: The user interface can be less polished than top competitors, and the automation builder isn't as flexible or powerful.
entries.criticism_short: Less polished UI and a basic automation builder.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-07.

entries.name: Omnisend
entries.best_for: Small to medium-sized e-commerce merchants looking for a powerful, easy-to-use alternative to Klaviyo with strong multi-channel capabilities.
entries.verdict: Omnisend makes the list at #10 as a strong, user-friendly contender in the e-commerce space, distinguishing itself with tightly integrated SMS and push notification channels within its automation workflows.
entries.verdict_short: A strong e-commerce platform that excels at integrating SMS and push notifications into automated workflows.
entries.praise: The ability to easily add SMS and push notifications into a single automation flow is a key strength for engaging mobile shoppers.
entries.praise_short: Seamlessly integrates email, SMS, and push.
entries.criticism: Its analytics and reporting are not as deep as Klaviyo's, and it lacks advanced features for B2B or non-e-commerce use cases.
entries.criticism_short: Analytics are less robust than top e-commerce rivals.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-07.

entries.name: GoHighLevel
entries.best_for: Marketing agencies that want to white-label and resell a comprehensive suite of marketing automation and sales tools to their own clients.
entries.verdict: Our wildcard #11, GoHighLevel, isn't for typical businesses but for agencies; it's a powerful, all-in-one platform designed to be white-labeled, allowing agencies to offer a full-fledged marketing automation service under their own brand.
entries.verdict_short: A unique, agency-focused platform that can be white-labeled and resold to multiple clients.
entries.praise: It packs an incredible amount of functionality—CRM, funnels, calendars, call tracking—into a single subscription that an agency can deploy across unlimited client accounts.
entries.praise_short: Massive feature set for a flat agency fee.
entries.criticism: The platform can be overwhelming and buggy at times, and as a jack-of-all-trades, individual features may not be as polished as standalone best-in-class tools.
entries.criticism_short: Overwhelming feature set can feel unpolished.

risk_signals.summary: No material public risk signals as of 2026-06-07.
